topic: NMP post-1990 acceleration — post-EXP-436 NP.xy cluster hoist verification
change_summary: detail_verdict_length shrank from 1226 to 751 (-38.7%); root cause is EXP-436 NP.xy cluster hoist (commit 795f3d1, 2026-05-29 02:45Z) compressing duplicated ~1000-char post-hoc paragraph in WIN-006/007/022 verdicts into a 1-sentence reference pointing to a new #posthoc-npxy worked-example anchor inside Part 3.1b (data/sections.json:74). Second smaller change in commit d68e153 (analyst-baby EXP-460 batch-5, 2026-05-30 15:40Z) tweaked WIN-007's reference text 'post-1990 acceleration target' → 'target number' per ISS-2294 hole-2 (the c1 part3-NPxy-EXP436 review flagged that monitor.py Domain #11 doesn't specifically test the post-1990 acceleration value — its longitude threshold and exponential-decay formula are shared with WIN-022). Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-31T01:14Z; obe_skipped=0; in_priority_queue=false.
our_argument_summary: WIN-007 (verdict 'Misleading') retains two structural arguments: (a) the 1990s NMP acceleration is real and well-documented (Chulliat & Newitt 2010 EOS; Livermore 2020 Nature Geoscience) but fully explained by competing flux-lobe dynamics on the core-mantle boundary, so the dome's 'aetheric threshold crossing' label adds no mechanism, derivation, or pre-prediction; (b) it fails our post-hoc test because the NOAA NGDC NP.xy series is an indexed public dataset and the dome's monitor.py hardcodes the target on the same commit as the live-fetch line. The (b) argument is now hoisted into the Part 3.1b worked-example cluster (data/sections.json #posthoc-npxy) rather than re-litigated in each of WIN-006/007/022's verdict prose. Verdict and TLDRs are unchanged from c3.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c3. NOAA NP.xy time-series (NMP position, drift 15→60 km/yr 1989→2002). Domain #11 longitude > 130°E test, pole at ~139°E — passes trivially. monitor.py not present in raw-text/ this cycle, same caveat as c3.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Unchanged: live-fetched pole position passed through a hardcoded scalar comparison (130°E) that any model would pass. Hoisted post-hoc cluster paragraph now characterizes this as 'monitor.py hardcodes the target value on the same commit as the live-fetch line' — semantically the same claim, just expressed at the cluster level. Both expressions are accurate descriptions of the same underlying repo structure.
data_sources_used: NOAA NP.xy time-series (NMP position, NOAA NGDC, gufm1+IGRF blend per cluster paragraph)
Livermore et al. 2020 Nat. Geosci. DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 (in detail_evidence; unchecked this cycle, verified in WIN-036.c3)
Chulliat & Newitt 2010 EOS DOI 10.1029/2010EO490001 (cited in both WIN-007 verdict reference and cluster paragraph; unchecked this cycle)
Newitt et al. 2002 DOI 10.1186/BF03352065 (in detail_evidence; unchecked this cycle)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None new. 'Phase transition' framing remains in detail_claim/detail_evidence; correctly flagged as category error.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The hoist preserves all code claims (live-fetch + hardcoded comparison + post-hoc adoption) — they are now expressed at the cluster level rather than per-WIN. The recent ISS-2294 patch ('post-1990 acceleration target' → 'target number') is a correctness improvement: monitor.py Domain #11 is shared between WIN-007 and WIN-022 (longitude > 130°E threshold + exponential-decay formula) and does not specifically encode a 'post-1990 acceleration' target value — saying 'target number' is more faithful to the actual repo content.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c3. monitor.py Domain #11 (per accumulated review history; monitor.py not present in raw-text/) fetches NOAA NP.xy live and compares longitude against a hardcoded scalar (130°E). Live fetch + hardcoded comparison. The ambiguity noted in c3 (defensible alternative reading 'hardcoded') persists; preserving 'live_fetch' for consistency with WIN-036/parallel WINs.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c3 (the c3 review verified the false→true flip applied in 2026-05-06 patch). Livermore 2020 provides the standard-physics flux-lobe explanation; dome renames the cause as 'aetheric vortex threshold crossing' with no different numerical prediction. Consistent with WIN-036 (same NP.xy data, same tag).
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c3. Detail_evidence explicitly cites Newitt et al. 2002 and Mandea & Dormy 2003 as pre-dating dome model existence. The hoist into the #posthoc-npxy cluster paragraph reinforces this — the cluster paragraph names the same indexing argument (Chulliat & Newitt 2010 EOS, publicly available long before any dome monitor commit).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c3. No derivation from dome geometry (disc_radius, firmament_height, sun/moon altitudes, aetheric parameters) produces the 1990 timing, the pre-1990 linear rate, or any post-1990 exponential constant. Dome states a label, not an equation.

holes_found.description: Stylistic awkwardness in the hoisted reference sentence: 'the asymmetry the author's own "timestamped before confirming data was pulled" definition requires' is a tight noun-phrase that omits a relative pronoun ('the asymmetry [that] the author's...'). The cluster paragraph at #posthoc-npxy uses the clearer phrasing 'the asymmetry between "prediction" and "confirmation" the author's own definition requires'. Readers who don't click through to the cluster see only the compressed form. Not factually wrong; just denser than necessary. The cluster paragraph's pre-hoist version (sections.json:74) is the cleaner read.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider mirroring the cluster paragraph's clearer wording: 'the asymmetry between "prediction" and "confirmation" the author's own definition requires cannot be checked from repository history'. Adds 8 words but eliminates the missing-relative-pronoun stumble. Decider: judgment call; not blocking.

holes_found.description: The 'monitor.py hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the live-fetch line' claim — repeated in both the hoisted cluster paragraph and (in compressed form) in WIN-007's verdict — cannot currently be re-verified from raw-text/ (no monitor.py in this cycle's raw-text/; same gap c3 flagged, WIN-036.c3 flagged, c2 had access). This is not a hole in WIN-007 specifically — it is the same systemic re-verification gap the c3 review carried over. Worth re-noting because the cluster hoist now makes monitor.py the load-bearing piece of evidence for THREE WINs (006/007/022) instead of one, so the gap's impact has grown. If monitor.py changes upstream and we don't pull it, all three WIN verdicts silently age.
holes_found.recommendation: Pull monitor.py back into raw-text/ at next refresh. This is becoming an integrity-agent concern more than a per-WIN concern; consider HOL- entry to enforce monitor.py freshness.

holes_found.description: Reader-path attack surface: a dome defender who reads only WIN-007's detail panel (without clicking through to #posthoc-npxy) now sees a shorter verdict that points to a cluster argument they may not follow. The first paragraph still carries the scientific verdict ('no mechanism, no derivation, no advance prediction'), so the WIN still stands on its own — but the post-hoc argument now requires a click to fully appreciate. Mitigation: the cross-reference text mentions 'Chulliat & Newitt 2010 EOS' and 'indexed public dataset' inline, so the gist survives skim-reading. This is a deliberate trade-off the hoist made (deduplication of ~1000 chars across three WINs vs. self-containment of each WIN's argument).
holes_found.recommendation: None — deliberate design choice. Flagging for the record so a future curmudgeon doesn't re-litigate it. If the deduplication ever causes a defender to land on WIN-007 and dismiss the post-hoc argument unread, that's a Part 3.1b TLDR improvement, not a WIN-007 issue.

stronger_arguments.description: The hoist exposes a stronger frame the hoisted cluster paragraph could exploit but does not: WIN-006/007/022 are not just 'three WINs that share a dataset' — they are three different rhetorical packagings of the SAME observation (NMP trajectory over 400 years). The cluster paragraph mentions inflation by naming the four WINs, but does not explicitly state 'the dome counts one observation as three confirmations.' This is a stronger version of the post-hoc critique: it is not just that the data was published first, but that the dome has triple-counted the same dataset under three different verdict styles (linear pre-1990 / phase-transition-at-1990 / acceleration-after-1990). Quoting from WIN-022's own detail_evidence: 'WIN-022 derives from the same parent prediction (PROS-002) as WIN-007 and uses identical NOAA NP.xy pole position data. This is not two independent confirmations — it is one observation counted twice under different labels.' That sentence belongs in the cluster paragraph too.
sources: data/wins.json WIN-022 detail_evidence (already says 'one observation counted twice under different labels')
data/wins.json WIN-006 detail_evidence (linear pre-1990 fit on same NOAA NP.xy)
data/wins.json WIN-007 detail_evidence (post-1990 acceleration on same NOAA NP.xy)
data/sections.json #posthoc-npxy cluster paragraph (currently names the four WINs but does not state the triple-counting)

stronger_arguments.description: The cluster paragraph at #posthoc-npxy notes WMM2025 'was released December 2024' but does not name the specific date for the Part 3.1b reader. PROS-006 was registered 2026-03-06 per raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41 (and WIN-036.c3 spot-check). That is 14 months between WMM2025 release and PROS-006 registration. Stating this explicitly in the cluster paragraph ('WMM2025 published the pole near 139°E in December 2024, 14 months before PROS-006 was registered on 2026-03-06') sharpens the WIN-036 OTS critique without bloating WIN-007's verdict.
sources: data/sections.json #posthoc-npxy (current text says 'more than fourteen months')

kernel_of_truth.description: Unchanged from c3 (no content change in the kernel-relevant fields). The dome correctly identifies and tracks a real, scientifically significant phenomenon: the NMP did accelerate sharply in the 1990s (data/wins.json WIN-007 detail_evidence: 'drift rates rising from ~15 km/yr in 1989 to ~60 km/yr by 2002', citing Chulliat & Newitt 2010, DOI 10.1029/2010EO490001 in the WIN-007 entry). The acceleration was significant enough that NOAA issued an out-of-cycle WMM update in 2019. Tracking the trajectory and registering a threshold (PROS-002 in raw-text/02-wins.txt:20) is engagement with real data, not random number-picking. The EXP-436 hoist (data/sections.json #posthoc-npxy) acknowledges this by naming the dataset family explicitly (NOAA NGDC, gufm1+IGRF reconstruction) rather than treating it as just 'some pole-position data.'
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Tracking a published phenomenon is not predicting it. Standard geophysics already attributes the acceleration to flux-lobe dynamics (data/wins.json WIN-007 detail_evidence — Livermore et al. 2020, DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9, Canadian-vs-Siberian lobe asymmetry). The dome offers no different numerical prediction — same trajectory, renamed cause ('aetheric vortex threshold crossing'). The cluster hoist actually strengthens this critique because it shows the post-hoc structural argument applies to a FAMILY of WINs (006/007/022/036), not just one — relabeling pattern at scale (data/sections.json #posthoc-npxy: 'Four WINs in this review... draw their confirming numbers from the same family of NOAA pole-position products').

deeper_analysis:
CYCLE 4 — POST-EXP-436 NP.xy CLUSTER HOIST VERIFICATION

TRIGGER: change-detected, drift-audit.json identified detail_verdict_length drift -38.7% (1226 → 751). No queue push; this is the change-detect catch.

WHAT CHANGED: Two commits between c3 (2026-05-06) and now (2026-05-31):
  1. 795f3d1 (2026-05-29 02:45Z) — decider integrated EXP-436 NP.xy cluster hoist. Compressed ~1000-char duplicated post-hoc paragraphs in WIN-006/007/022 verdicts to 1-sentence references pointing to new #posthoc-npxy worked-example anchor in Part 3.1b. WIN-036 explicitly NOT modified (different post-hoc argument running through OTS canary). Resolved ISS-2214 (c6 minor: duplicated post-hoc paragraph TF-IDF dilution).
  2. d68e153 (2026-05-30 15:40Z) — analyst-baby EXP-460 batch-5 patched WIN-007 ISS-2294 hole-2: 'post-1990 acceleration target' → 'target number'. Per the c1 review of part3-NPxy-EXP436, monitor.py Domain #11 doesn't specifically test the post-1990 acceleration value — its longitude threshold + exponential-decay formula are shared with WIN-022, so the more generic 'target number' is more truthful.

VERDICT MISMATCH CHECK: Verdict 'Misleading' continues to match the evidence. Detail_evidence (unchanged at 2011 chars) still describes the post-hoc adoption + category-error 'phase transition' + Livermore-explained mechanism. TLDR-verdict (unchanged) still says 'fully explained by standard flux lobe dynamics... only a post-hoc label applied after the data was published.' First paragraph of detail_verdict_text (also unchanged) reiterates this. No verdict drift.

HOIST QUALITY ASSESSMENT: The hoist is a clean refactor. The compressed reference in WIN-007 names the dataset (NOAA NGDC NP.xy), the citation (Chulliat & Newitt 2010 EOS), and the core argumentative structure (hardcoded target on same commit as live-fetch). The cluster paragraph at #posthoc-npxy carries the full argument with additional context (gufm1+IGRF blend, WMM2025 vs PROS-006 timing). The two are consistent: the cluster says 'For WIN-006 and WIN-007 the dome's monitor.py hardcodes the target value on the same commit as the live-fetch line, so the asymmetry between "prediction" and "confirmation" the author's own definition requires cannot be checked from repository history' — WIN-007 says the same thing more tightly. WIN-007 also correctly carries the 'fails our post-hoc test' clause that signals what the verdict-relevant conclusion of the cluster argument is, so the verdict still reads coherently even if the reader doesn't click through.

ROBUSTNESS OF THE ISS-2294 TWEAK: The 'target number' wording is faithful to repo reality. Domain #11's longitude > 130°E check + 55.0·exp(−0.08·(year−2015)) formula are shared between WIN-007 and WIN-022 (per WIN-022 c-history). Calling it the 'post-1990 acceleration target' was overspecific; 'target number' captures the right level of generality. Good patch.

ADVOCATE MODE (steel-man): A capable dome defender might argue 'the hoist itself is suspicious — you compressed three independent confirmations into a single "cluster" critique because you wanted to deflate the WIN count.' Rebuttal: the dome itself groups these WINs by parent prediction PROS-002 (raw-text/02-wins.txt:20-22 names WIN-007 and W010/WIN-036 as PROS-002 outputs) and uses the same NP.xy data family for all four (per the cluster paragraph). Treating them as a cluster is a reflection of the dome's own structural choice, not editorial gerrymandering. Defense rating: 2/5 (procedurally serious but factually weak — dome's own structure supports the cluster framing).

CROSS-WIN CONSISTENCY (post-hoist):
  - WIN-006 (linear pre-1990 drift): verdict 'Std Model Explains' length 2640, includes a verdict-boundary note explaining why it's not 'Misleading'. Cluster reference present.
  - WIN-007 (post-1990 acceleration claim, this review): verdict 'Misleading' length 751. Cluster reference present.
  - WIN-022 (phase transition relabeling): verdict 'Misleading' length 862. Cluster reference present (with WIN-022-specific addendum that there's no dedicated monitor.py entry).
  - WIN-036 (NMP > 18° east of 120°E): verdict 'Std Model Explains' length 1926. NOT cluster-referenced (different post-hoc path via OTS canary).
  All four code_analysis tags are consistent with the cluster argument and with each other on the shared dimensions (post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false). Monitoring differs across the four (live_fetch for 006/007; none for 022; hardcoded for 036) — those are accurate per the actual code paths. No cross-WIN contradiction.

QUANTITATIVE VERIFICATION: No new numerical claims in the changed text. Existing numbers (pre-1990 0.0466 deg/year, post-1990 ~60 km/yr by 2002, threshold > 130°E, pole at 139.298°E) are in detail_evidence (unchanged) or in the cluster paragraph (verifiable: 0.0466·400=18.64°; 19.298° > 18°). Numerically self-consistent; no drift.

NET ASSESSMENT: The hoist is a good refactor. It reduces duplication, makes the post-hoc argument's cluster-scope explicit, and preserves the per-WIN verdicts' integrity. The ISS-2294 follow-up patch is correct on the merits. The three minor holes flagged are stylistic/integrity-level (clearer wording in the compressed reference; monitor.py freshness; reader-path attack surface) — none demand a verdict change or major rewrite. recommended_action: no_change.

monitor.py Domain #11 — not present in raw-text/ this cycle (same caveat as c3 and WIN-036.c3); the hoist's 'hardcoded target on same commit as live-fetch' assertion rests on accumulated prior-cycle evidence, not this-cycle re-verification

advocate_mode.best_defense: A capable dome defender's strongest line on the hoisted form: 'Your verdict is now a forward-reference. A reader who lands on WIN-007 sees a one-paragraph scientific complaint plus a click-through to a Part 3.1b paragraph that lumps four WINs together. The reader is being asked to follow a structural argument applied to a cluster, not to weigh the WIN-007-specific evidence. This is editorial sleight-of-hand: critique by category, not by content. The original c3-era verdict at least stated the WIN-007-specific timestamp/commit argument inline; you have now buried it.' Secondary line: 'The cluster paragraph's claim that monitor.py hardcodes the target on the same commit as the live-fetch is unverifiable from your own raw-text/ this cycle, so the post-hoc argument's evidentiary base is now load-bearing on cached prior-cycle observations rather than fresh code inspection.'
advocate_mode.rebuttal: (1) The cluster framing is justified by the dome's own structural choice (PROS-002 → WIN-007 + WIN-036; same NOAA NP.xy data family across WIN-006/007/022). Treating these as a cluster reflects the dome's grouping, not editorial repackaging. (2) WIN-007's compressed reference still names the WIN-specific scientific verdict ('no mechanism, no derivation, no advance prediction' — first paragraph, unchanged from c3) — the click-through is for the structural post-hoc evidence, not for the verdict itself. (3) The monitor.py freshness gap is real (flagged as a separate minor hole) but it pre-dates the hoist; it is a re-verification cycle issue, not a hoist-induced regression.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: NONE post-hoist. The four WINs in the NP.xy cluster (WIN-006/007/022/036) carry consistent code_analysis tags on the shared dimensions (post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false, relabels_standard=true) and only differ on monitoring (live_fetch / live_fetch / none / hardcoded), which is correct per the actual code paths. The cluster paragraph's '#posthoc-npxy' anchor is referenced by WIN-006/007/022's verdicts; WIN-036 is correctly treated separately (its post-hoc failure runs through the OTS canary, not the hardcoded-monitor pattern). The verdict-boundary note added to WIN-006 (length 2640, includes explanation of why it carries 'Std Model Explains' rather than 'Misleading' despite shared mechanism framing) closes the only remaining cross-WIN gap on this cluster.

claims_checked: detail_verdict_length drift: c3 fingerprint 1226 → current 751 = -38.7% (matches drift-audit.json's reported magnitude; sign confirmed negative, i.e., shortened, not lengthened)
Pre-1990 0.0466 deg/year × 400 years = 18.64° (cluster paragraph's implicit 400-year pre-1990 backdrop)
Pole at 139.298°E - 120°E = 19.298° > 18° threshold (WIN-036 trigger condition, cluster-relevant)
WMM2025 December 2024 to PROS-006 March 2026 = ~14 months (cluster paragraph's 'more than fourteen months' claim)
quantitative_verification.computations: length-drift computed by direct character comparison between c3 review's text_fingerprint.detail_verdict_length (1226) and current detail_verdict_text length (751); pre-1990 cumulative drift = 0.0466 × 400 = 18.64; longitude deviation = 139.298 − 120 = 19.298; WMM-to-PROS gap ≈ 14 months by calendar arithmetic.

summary_for_decider: WIN-007 c4 verifies the EXP-436 NP.xy cluster hoist (commit 795f3d1) + ISS-2294 follow-up patch (commit d68e153). Verdict 'Misleading' unchanged. Detail_verdict_text shortened 1226 → 751 chars via hoist of the post-hoc paragraph into Part 3.1b #posthoc-npxy worked-example cluster anchor — clean refactor, no factual or argumentative regression. Three minor holes flagged: (1) stylistic awkwardness in the compressed reference's noun-phrase 'the asymmetry the author's own definition requires' — cluster paragraph's clearer wording could be mirrored, judgment call; (2) monitor.py absence from raw-text/ persists from c3 — the cluster hoist now makes monitor.py load-bearing for THREE WINs instead of one, so the re-verification gap's impact has grown; (3) reader-path attack surface from converting in-WIN argument to forward-reference — deliberate design choice, flagged for record. No verdict change recommended. Stronger-arguments lens surfaces two cluster-paragraph improvement candidates (explicit 'one observation counted three times' framing; explicit WMM2025-Dec-2024 vs PROS-006-Mar-2026 timing). Both are sections.json patches, not wins.json patches — not in scope for WIN-007 verdict, route to Part 3.1b refinement if useful. recommended_action: no_change.
